Integration REST API runs as scheduled Azure Function App.

instructionSteps.description: >**NOTE:** The ESET Protect Platform data connector uses Azure Functions to connect to the ESET Protect Platform via Eset Connect API to pull detections logs into Microsoft Sentinel. This process might result in additional data ingestion costs. See details on the [Azure Functions pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/).

>**NOTE:** The newest version of the ESET PROTECT Platform and Microsoft Sentinel integration pulls not only detections logs but also newly created incidents. If your integration was set up before 20.06.2025, please follow [these steps](https://help.eset.com/eset_connect/en-US/update_ms_sentinel_integration.html) to update it.
Use this [instruction](https://help.eset.com/eset_connect/en-US/create_api_user_account.html) to create an ESET Connect API User account with **Login** and **Password**.
instructionSteps.title: Step 1 -  Create an API user

instructionSteps.description: Create a Microsoft Entra ID registered application by following the steps in the [Register a new application instruction.](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/quickstart-register-app)
instructionSteps.title: Step 2 -  Create a registered application

instructionSteps.description:
1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below. 

	[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://aka.ms/sentinel-EsetProtectionPlatform-azuredeploy)

2. Select the name of the **Log Analytics workspace** associated with your Microsoft Sentinel. Select the same **Resource Group** as the Resource Group of the Log Analytics workspace.

3. Type the parameters of the registered application in Microsoft Entra ID: **Azure Client ID**, **Azure Client Secret**, **Azure Tenant ID**, **Object ID**. You can find the **Object ID** on Azure Portal by following this path 
> Microsoft Entra ID -> Manage (on the left-side menu) -> Enterprise applications -> Object ID column (the value next to your registered application name).

4. Provide the ESET Connect API user account **Login** and **Password** obtained in **Step 1**.

5. Select one or more ESET products (ESET PROTECT, ESET Inspect, ESET Cloud Office Security) from which detections are retrieved.
instructionSteps.title: Step 3 - Deploy the ESET Protect Platform data connector using the Azure Resource Manager (ARM) template

1. Click the **Deploy to Azure** button below. 

	[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://aka.ms/sentinel-EsetProtectionPlatform-azuredeploy)

2. Select the name of the **Log Analytics workspace** associated with your Microsoft Sentinel. Select the same **Resource Group** as the Resource Group of the Log Analytics workspace.

3. Type the parameters of the registered application in Microsoft Entra ID: **Azure Client ID**, **Azure Client Secret**, **Azure Tenant ID**, **Object ID**. You can find the **Object ID** on Azure Portal by following this path 
> Microsoft Entra ID -> Manage (on the left-side menu) -> Enterprise applications -> Object ID column (the value next to your registered application name).

properties.displayName: ESET Connect Data Connector (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorUiConfig.title: ESET Connect Data Connector (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The ESET Connect data connector enables comprehensive security monitoring by ingesting threat detections and incident data from multiple ESET products through the ESET Connect REST API. All detections from ESET PROTECT, ESET Inspect, and ESET Cloud Office Security are unified into the Detections table, with ESET PROTECT and Inspect providing basic endpoint detections via the /v1/detections API, while ESET Cloud Office Security delivers enhanced email security detections through the /v2/detections API. Additionally, incident management data from ESET Inspect is collected into the Incidents table via the /v2/incidents endpoint, providing enhanced incident correlation and management capabilities. The connector uses ESET Connect API credentials with username and password authentication, requiring an API user account with appropriate permissions for your selected products. Before using this connector, ensure you have an ESET Connect API user account, access to your selected ESET products (EP/EI/ECOS), appropriate API permissions for data access, and note that ESET Cloud Office Security is not supported in the Japan region.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Connector Management
instructionSteps.description: Manage your ESET Connect connector instances

parameters.content:
## ESET Connect Instances

Manage multiple ESET Connect connector instances. Each instance can monitor different ESET products and connect to different environments or regions.

## Product Selection

Select which ESET product this connector instance will monitor. Create separate instances for each product you want to monitor.

parameters.text:
**Product Details:**
- **ESET PROTECT**: Endpoint detections via /v1/detections
- **ESET Inspect**: Detections via /v1/detections + Incidents via /v2/incidents
- **ESET Cloud Office Security**: Email security detections via /v2/detections (not available in Japan region)

parameters.content:
## API Configuration

Configure your ESET Connect API endpoint and credentials.
> **Optional:** Specify a detection ID to start from. Leave empty to collect all detections. This filter is applied in addition to the automatic time-based tracking.

parameters.text:
**Important**: Enter the regional ESET Connect API base URL (not the portal URL). Common examples:
- United States: `https://us.incident-management.eset.systems`
- Europe: `https://eu.incident-management.eset.systems`
- Japan: `https://jpn.incident-management.eset.systems`
- On-Premise ESET Inspect: Enter your server URL (e.g., `https://your-server:8443`)

parameters.content:
## Authentication

Provide your ESET Connect API credentials.

parameters.label: ESET API Username
parameters.placeholder: Enter your ESET Connect API username (email)
parameters.name: username

parameters.label: ESET API Password
parameters.placeholder: Enter your ESET Connect API password
parameters.name: password

parameters.text: **Client ID/Secret**: ESET ignores these. Enter any non-empty values.
**ECOS Requirement**: If using ESET Cloud Office Security, you must log into the ECOS instance with these API credentials at least once before data collection will work.

parameters.label: Connector Friendly Name
parameters.placeholder: Enter a unique friendly name for this connector
parameters.name: friendlyName

parameters.text: The friendly name helps you identify this connector instance in the grid and in the collected data.
**Data Collection Schedule**: The connector will poll for new data every 15 minutes per selected product.

columns.name: TimeGenerated

columns.name: DetectionType
columns.description: Type of detection (numeric: 0=UnknownAlarm, 1=RuleActivated, 2=MalwareFoundOnDisk, 3=MalwareFoundInMemory, 4=ExploitDetected, 5=FirewallDetection, 7=BlockedAddress, 8=CryptoBlockerDetection)

columns.name: UserName

columns.name: Device
columns.description: Device object with displayName and uuid (v2 API only)

columns.name: Email
columns.description: Email object with attachments, headers, sender, recipient (v2 API only)

columns.name: File
columns.description: File object with path, hashes, size (v2 API only)

columns.name: NetworkTraffic
columns.description: Network traffic object with source/destination details (v2 API only)

columns.name: Process
columns.description: Process object with commandLine, path, uuid (v2 API only)

columns.name: NetworkCommunication
columns.description: Network communication details (v1 API)

columns.name: DetectionUuid
columns.description: Detection UUID (mapped from uuid for compatibility)

columns.name: Context
columns.description: Context information about the detection (v1 API)

columns.name: GroupSize
columns.description: Number of similar detections grouped together

columns.name: SeverityScore
columns.description: Severity score as string for backward compatibility. DEPRECATED: for SeverityScoreInt

columns.name: SeverityScoreInt
columns.description: Severity score as integer (correct API type)

columns.name: DeviceDisplayName
columns.description: Display name of the device (v2: device.displayName object; v1: not available in payload)

columns.name: DeviceUuid
columns.description: UUID of the device (v2: device.uuid object; v1: context.deviceUuid)

columns.name: UserNameBase
columns.description: Base username associated with the detection

columns.name: ProcessPath
columns.description: Path of the process involved (v2 direct field, v1 nested in context.process.path)

columns.name: ProcessUuid
columns.description: UUID of the process

columns.name: ProcessCommandline
columns.description: Process command line

columns.name: ObjectSizeBytes
columns.description: Size of the detected object in bytes as integer for backward compatibility. DEPRECATED: for ObjectSizeBytesString

columns.name: ObjectSizeBytesString
columns.description: Size of the detected object in bytes as string (correct API type)

columns.name: EdrRuleUuid
columns.description: EDR rule UUID that triggered the detection

columns.name: Note
columns.description: Additional notes about the detection

columns.name: Resolved
columns.description: Whether the detection has been resolved

columns.name: CloudOfficeTenantUuid
columns.description: Cloud office tenant UUID

columns.name: ScanUuid
columns.description: Scan session UUID

columns.name: TriggeringEvent
columns.description: Event that triggered the detection

connectorUiConfig.title: ESET Connect Data Connector (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The ESET Connect data connector enables comprehensive security monitoring by ingesting threat detections and incident data from multiple ESET products through the ESET Connect REST API. All detections from ESET PROTECT, ESET Inspect, and ESET Cloud Office Security are unified into the Detections table, with ESET PROTECT and Inspect providing basic endpoint detections via the /v1/detections API, while ESET Cloud Office Security delivers enhanced email security detections through the /v2/detections API. Additionally, incident management data from ESET Inspect is collected into the Incidents table via the /v2/incidents endpoint, providing enhanced incident correlation and management capabilities. The connector uses ESET Connect API credentials with username and password authentication, requiring an API user account with appropriate permissions for your selected products. Before using this connector, ensure you have an ESET Connect API user account, access to your selected ESET products (EP/EI/ECOS), appropriate API permissions for data access, and note that ESET Cloud Office Security is not supported in the Japan region.

| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h) | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)

instructionSteps.title: 1. Connector Management
instructionSteps.description: Manage your ESET Connect connector instances

parameters.content:
## ESET Connect Instances

Manage multiple ESET Connect connector instances. Each instance can monitor different ESET products and connect to different environments or regions.

## Product Selection

Select which ESET product this connector instance will monitor. Create separate instances for each product you want to monitor.

parameters.text:
**Product Details:**
- **ESET PROTECT**: Endpoint detections via /v1/detections
- **ESET Inspect**: Detections via /v1/detections + Incidents via /v2/incidents
- **ESET Cloud Office Security**: Email security detections via /v2/detections (not available in Japan region)

parameters.content:
## API Configuration

Configure your ESET Connect API endpoint and credentials.
> **Optional:** Specify a detection ID to start from. Leave empty to collect all detections. This filter is applied in addition to the automatic time-based tracking.

parameters.text:
**Important**: Enter the regional ESET Connect API base URL (not the portal URL). Common examples:
- United States: `https://us.incident-management.eset.systems`
- Europe: `https://eu.incident-management.eset.systems`
- Japan: `https://jpn.incident-management.eset.systems`
- On-Premise ESET Inspect: Enter your server URL (e.g., `https://your-server:8443`)

parameters.content:
## Authentication

Provide your ESET Connect API credentials.

parameters.label: ESET API Username
parameters.placeholder: Enter your ESET Connect API username (email)
parameters.name: username

parameters.label: ESET API Password
parameters.placeholder: Enter your ESET Connect API password
parameters.name: password

parameters.text: **Client ID/Secret**: ESET ignores these. Enter any non-empty values.
**ECOS Requirement**: If using ESET Cloud Office Security, you must log into the ECOS instance with these API credentials at least once before data collection will work.

parameters.label: Connector Friendly Name
parameters.placeholder: Enter a unique friendly name for this connector
parameters.name: friendlyName

parameters.text: The friendly name helps you identify this connector instance in the grid and in the collected data.
**Data Collection Schedule**: The connector will poll for new data every 15 minutes per selected product.

properties.displayName: ESET Connect Data Connector (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorDefinitionName.defaultValue: ESET Connect Data Connector (via Codeless Connector Framework)

nextSteps.stepPlaceholdersParsingKql: source | project res = parse_json(data) | project detections = res['value'] | mvexpand detections | project id = detections['id']

properties.description: ESETProtectPlatform Data Parser with template version 3.0.0

properties.query:
union isfuzzy=true IntegrationTable_CL, IntegrationTableV2_CL
    | extend
        networkCommunication = column_ifexists('NetworkCommunication', column_ifexists('networkCommunication', dynamic(null))),
        severityLevel = tostring(column_ifexists('SeverityLevel', column_ifexists('severityLevel', ''))),
        occurTime = column_ifexists('OccurTime', column_ifexists('occurTime', ''))
    | extend 
        EventResult = "NA",
        EventProduct = "ESET Connect",
        EventVendor = "ESET",
        DvcIdType = "Computer",
        EventEndTime = column_ifexists('TimeGenerated', ''),
        SrcIpAddr = tostring(networkCommunication['localIpAddress']),
        DstIpAddr = networkCommunication['remoteIpAddress'],
        SrcPortNumber = toint(networkCommunication['localPort']),
        DstPortNumber = networkCommunication['remotePort'],
        NetworkDirection = case(
            networkCommunication['direction'] == "NETWORK_COMMUNICATION_DIRECTION_UNSPECIFIED", "NA",
            networkCommunication['direction'] == "NETWORK_COMMUNICATION_DIRECTION_INBOUND", "Inbound",
            "Outbound"
        ),
        NetworkProtocol = networkCommunication['protocolName'],
        EventStartTime = todatetime(occurTime), 
        EventSeverity = case(
            severityLevel == "SEVERITY_LEVEL_UNSPECIFIED", "Informational",
            severityLevel == "SEVERITY_LEVEL_LOW", "Low",
            severityLevel == "SEVERITY_LEVEL_MEDIUM", "Medium",
            severityLevel == "SEVERITY_LEVEL_HIGH", "High",
            "Unknown"
        ),
        EventMessage = column_ifexists('DisplayName', column_ifexists('displayName', '')),
        EventOriginalUid = column_ifexists('DetectionUuid', column_ifexists('detectionUuid', '')),
        EventCount = column_ifexists('GroupSize', column_ifexists('groupSize', int(null))),
        EventOriginalSeverity = column_ifexists('SeverityScore', column_ifexists('severityScore', '')),
        EventOriginalType = column_ifexists('Category', column_ifexists('category', '')),
        EventType = column_ifexists('TypeName', column_ifexists('typeName', '')),
        DvcDescription = column_ifexists('DeviceDisplayName', column_ifexists('deviceDisplayName', '')),
        DvcId = column_ifexists('DeviceUuid', column_ifexists('deviceUuid', '')),
        ActorUsername = column_ifexists('UserNameBase', column_ifexists('userNameBase', '')),
        TargetProcessCurrentDirectory = column_ifexists('ProcessPath', column_ifexists('processPath', '')),
        TargetProcessGuid = column_ifexists('ProcessUuid', column_ifexists('processUuid', '')),
        TargetProcessCommandLine = column_ifexists('ProcessCommandline', column_ifexists('processCommandline', '')),
        TargetProcessFileSize = column_ifexists('ObjectSizeBytes', column_ifexists('objectSizeBytes', ''))
    | extend 
        Dvc = DvcId,
        User = ActorUsername
    | project-away
        severityLevel, networkCommunication, occurTime

tags.name: description
tags.value: Parser for ESET Protect Platform detections. Normalizes data from both the legacy Azure Functions connector (IntegrationTable_CL) and the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) connector (IntegrationTableV2_CL) so that downstream content works regardless of which data connector is deployed.

tags.name: description
tags.value: Parser for ESET Protect Platform detections. Normalizes data from both the legacy Azure Functions connector (IntegrationTable_CL) and the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) connector (IntegrationTableV2_CL) so that downstream content works regardless of which data connector is deployed.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

ESET Protect Platform solution for Microsoft Sentinel ingests detections from ESET Protect Platform using the provided Integration REST API.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

The ESET Protect Platform solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  1. Logs Ingestion API in Azure Monitor

  2. Azure Functions

Data Connectors: 2, Parsers: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
